name: find
context: Query MongoDB collections with filters, projections, sort, skip, limit, and cursor-aware reads
info: This sub-skill focuses on read paths: `find()` and `findOne()`, how filters select documents, how projections and sort shape the response, and how cursor-based iteration differs from pulling everything into memory. Agents use it for targeted inspection, audits, pagination, and export jobs where query shape matters as much as the result.

sections.title: Filters and projection
sections.body: Filters determine which documents match. Projection determines which fields are returned. Combining both keeps reads precise and cheap. This is especially important when documents are large or contain nested arrays that the caller does not need.

sections.title: Sort, skip, and limit
sections.body: Sort before limit when you need deterministic top-N results. Use skip for offset pagination only on modest result sets; it becomes expensive at high offsets. For high-volume pagination, prefer a stable range query on an indexed field such as `_id` or `createdAt`.

sections.title: Cursor behavior
sections.body: A cursor streams results from the server. `toArray()` is convenient when the result set is bounded. For exports or scans, iterate the cursor and process incrementally so the agent does not load the entire collection into memory at once.

links.context: Parent CRUD skill for the broader document lifecycle
Add or inspect indexes when reads are slow or use sort frequently
Escalate to aggregation when query-time reshaping becomes complex

related.note: Good query performance depends on indexes that support the filter and sort shape
Use aggregation when a plain find no longer expresses the read task cleanly

scripts.name: Find documents
scripts.description: Run a focused MongoDB read query with filter, projection, sort, skip, limit, and hint options

FIND_INPUT_JSON.description: Extended JSON payload. If omitted, the script reads JSON from stdin.
